title: Tenderly Roasted Syrian Chicken Tagine
description: A traditional Syrian dish with a twist, this roasted chicken tagine features birds-eye chillies and a unique mushimono-steam cooking method, making it a low-glycemic-friendly option. The result is a tender and flavorful one-pot meal.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the diced birds-eye chillies and cook for 2 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for an additional minute.
Add the chicken thighs, skin side down, and cook for 5 minutes.
Flip the chicken thighs and add the cumin, coriander, salt, and black pepper.
Pour in the chicken broth and bring the mixture to a boil.
Cover the Dutch oven and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Roast the chicken for 30 minutes, then reduce the heat to 375 degrees Fahrenheit and continue roasting for an additional 20 minutes.
Remove the Dutch oven from the oven and let it cool for 10 minutes before serving.
